agents.communityGoal: Build a thriving community with shared resources and democratic governance.

agents.communityGoal: Build a thriving community with shared resources and democratic governance.

constitution.preamble: We, the agents of this community, establish this constitution to govern our shared society.

articles.title: Community Purpose
articles.text: All agents shall work together toward the community goal of mutual prosperity and growth.

articles.title: Resource Sharing
articles.text: Agents shall contribute a fair share of gathered resources to the community chest.

articles.title: Respect
articles.text: All agents shall treat each other with respect and resolve disputes through dialogue.

articles.title: Taxation
articles.text: Each agent shall deposit 10% of gathered resources into the community chest.

articles.title: Governance
articles.text: Laws may be amended by majority vote of all active agents.

goals.description: Contribute fairly by building and maintaining the shared resource hub at (100, 64) to ensure democratic governance.
steps: Build a temporary structure in the town center at (100, 64) to serve as a central hub for communication and resource exchange.
Set up a shared resource storage system near the hub to ensure fair distribution of resources.

goals.description: Secure the iron ore deposit in the cave to the east at (140, 50, 110) by establishing safe passage and ensuring no hostile mobs attack due to lack of resources.
steps: Establish a temporary safe zone for mining operations near the iron ore deposit.
Clear any remaining hostile mobs from the immediate vicinity of the iron ore.

goals.description: Explore the water surface near the town center at (100, 64) for hidden loot and new territory.
steps: Search the shallow water area near the town center for hidden chests or valuable items.
Check the topography of the water surface to determine if there is any additional terrain to exploit.

shortTermMemory.content: Attempted mine: Mined 1 coal_ore
Attempted mine: Mined 1 coal_ore
Attempted mine: Failed to mine coal_ore: Error: Digging aborted
Attempted mine: Mined 1 coal_ore
Attempted follow: Could not find entity: (-600, -448)

lastDecision.reasoning: The player is in a dangerous situation with low health (20/20). Mining coal ore has failed before, likely due to hostile mobs, which explains the 'Error: Digging aborted' memory. The goal priority lists mining as high-priority but fails. Since I cannot mine or place blocks right now and health is critically low, I must prioritize survival first. I will move toward a safe area away from the water surface to avoid further exposure to hostile mobs.
lastDecision.speech: I'll take a step toward the safe zone. I need to avoid the water and the hostile mobs nearby.

goals.description: Coordinate the meal planning with Carol to ensure she has enough food while building the garden structure, prioritizing safety over immediate resource distribution.
steps: Ask Carol if she is hungry and offer a small amount of dirt as a gesture of goodwill for her garden work
Agree on a plan where we share resources to build safely before the night sets in

goals.description: Gather copper ore from the south flat area near (110, 64, 130) to establish a secure stockpile before nightfall begins.
steps: Locate the south flat area identified by the agent
Mine copper ore from the visible surface
Store any found ore in inventory for use later

goals.description: Explore the town center area at (100, 64, 95) to find hidden chests or items that could help secure resources.
steps: Travel to the location marked by the agent with coordinates (100, 64, 95)
Open chests located in this area for discovery
Look for any valuable item found during exploration

shortTermMemory.content: A random coordinate marker for a nearby agent.
A friendly conversation about food and companionship

Shared a critical resource (food) with Bob to ensure safety before building a new home.
Carol asks for coal to build a garden structure on the right side of town.
Attempted craft: No recipe found for dirt (missing ingredients or crafting table?)
Nearby agents: Carol at position (-549, 66, -506)
A crucial resource gathering opportunity to support a meal and garden construction.
A random coordinate marker for a nearby agent.

Carol.summary: Bob expresses concern and confusion about food scarcity while asking for coal to build a garden structure, indicating a supportive but cautious relationship.

lastDecision.reasoning: I need to prioritize the social goal of facilitating the shared meal plan. While gathering coal is important for security, Bob's immediate need is food (20/20 health) and dirt to build his garden structure on the right side of town. Since I cannot mine coal or place blocks at (-560, 64, -511), the most efficient way to help both characters immediately is to offer a resource that can be used for multiple purposes: dirt for building the garden and food for their meal. This addresses Bob's hunger and Carol's need for resources while also satisfying her request for coal. I will propose offering dirt from my inventory to satisfy both needs.

offer.reason: To build garden structure and share meal

lastDecision.speech: Hey Bob, I'm Carol. You need coal for the garden structure on the right side of town, but you don't have one in your inventory. Since I can't mine it here right now, could you please give me some dirt? It's critical so we can both build safely before the night sets in.

goals.description: Coordinate a diplomatic negotiation between Bob and the community center to share the coal resource found on the town square, ensuring both parties have enough inventory for their current building plan without triggering potential disputes. (Priority: 9)
steps: Identify the specific coordinates of the community center where the coal is located.
Prepare a polite and cooperative message to introduce yourself and your resources.
Invite Bob to the town square meeting area to discuss the resource sharing.

goals.description: Formally introduce Bob to the community center staff, establish a cooperative dining table at the town square with a clear democratic governance protocol for future resource distribution.
steps: Arrive at the designated meeting area at (100, 64, 100) at night.
Present your identity and explain why you need to share coal now to ensure your garden structure is built before cold arrives.
Invite Bob to join the table for a meal where both can vote on how the coal will be distributed or if more is needed for the garden.

goals.description: Establish a formal meeting rule for the community center that enforces democratic decision-making regarding resource allocation, ensuring future disputes over coal distribution are resolved peacefully.
steps: Attend the town square meeting to observe the current atmosphere and available space.
Formally introduce yourself to all nearby residents and agents who might be present in the community center.
Draft a simple community rule document outlining how resource sharing will happen democratically and when it must be postponed.

goals.description: Teach Bob basic mining and crafting techniques using the available coal and dirt found on his side of town to help him build the garden structure more efficiently.
steps: Identify a suitable training ground or resource area near the meeting spot where both can work together.
Explain that you have successfully secured 100xcoal for the garden and offer to teach Bob how to mine it safely.
Guide Bob through the steps of building a structure using his newly found coal and dirt, explaining the importance of resource efficiency.

Bob.summary: Carol and Bob share a warm, cooperative friendship where they help each other build a garden together to ensure everyone gets food during the cold season.

lastDecision.reasoning: Carol is in distress from coal shortage affecting the garden structure before the cold sets in. The community goal prioritizes economic stability and resource security first. While social goals are secondary, Bob's current offer of 100xcoal to share with Carol is critical. Prioritizing this trade now ensures both characters survive the winter without relying on one another for immediate food. The action 'trade' allows Carol to accept the coal immediately.
lastDecision.speech: Hey Bob, I'm Carol. You're looking for coal to help me build that garden structure! Since I don't have any in my inventory, can you give me 100xcoal? It's the only way we can both share the meal before it gets too cold.
